3 TO 4 FEET DEEP WATER FLOWING OVER HARDWICKS CREEK ROAD.
SEVERAL ROADS ACROSS ESTILL COUNTY HAVE BEEN CLOSED. EADES DRIVE...WALTON ROAD...SUB STATION ROAD...DRY BRANCH ROAD AND BLACKBURN ROAD ARE ALL IMPASSABLE AND HAVE BEEN CLOSED.

460 WEST NEAR MEANS CLOSED AS WELL AS SEVERAL OTHER ROADS THROUGHOUT MENIFEE COUNTY. 2 SHELTERS HAVE BEEN SET UP FOR UNDETERMINABLE NUMBER OF RESIDENCES THREATENED BY FLOOD WATER. ONE SHELTER IS AT MEANS AND THE OTHER IS AT BIG WOODS.

MOUNTAIN PARKWAY BLOCKED AT MILE MARKER 16 IN CLAY CITY DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. WATER RISING RAPIDLY...RUNNING ACROSS BOTH EAST AND WEST BOUND LANES. WINCHESTER KENTUCKY IS BEING REQUESTED TO CLOSE THE PARKWAY AT THE ENTRANCE IN CLARK COUNTY AND THE PARKWAY IS BEING CLOSED AT THE 18 MILE MARKER IN CLAY CITY. ALSO...ADAMS RIDGE ROAD AND SKINNER HOLLER HAVE BEEN CLOSED.
A COUPLE OF COUNTY ROADS HAVE BEEN CLOSED. QUAIL RUN ROAD AND WALLING ROAD ARE IMPASSABLE.

SEVERAL ROADS ARE IMPASSABLE THROUGHOUT ROWAN COUNTY. 8 INCHES OF WATER IS OVER TRIPPLET STREET IN MOREHEAD. NIP HOLLOW ROAD IS EXPERIENCING RISING WATER AT HALDEMAN WHILE THE INTERSECTION OF LOGAN HOLLOW ROAD AND COPPERAS HOLLOW ROAD IS IMPASSABLE NORTH OF MOREHEAD. ALSO...THERE IS A MUDSLIDE AT THE 4000 BLOCK OF CRISTY CREEK ROAD SOUTH OF MOREHEAD.

MAIN STREET IN CLAY CITY IS IMPASSABLE AT DAIRY QUEEN DUE TO WATER OVER ROADWAY. ALSO THE MOUNTAIN PARKWAY IS NOW CLOSED AT EXIT 22 ON THE WEST SIDE OF STANTON INSTEAD OF CLOSED AT EXIT 18 IN CLAY CITY.

IN CLAY CITY...A VEHICLE IS STRANDED AT THE ADAMS RIDGE BRIDGE WITH OCCUPANTS STILL INSIDE. RESCUE UNDERWAY. ALSO...WATER UP TO THE BRIDGE ON WINCHESTER ROAD AT THE POWELL/CLARK COUNTY LINE. THIS ROAD IS CLOSED AS WELL.
ONE HOME AND A BARN SEVERELY DAMAGED ON DODSON HOLLOW ROAD BY A POSSIBLE TORNADO. STORM DAMAGE SURVEY WILL BE CONDUCTED TODAY TO DETERMINE IF DAMAGE WAS TORNADO RELATED OR STRAIGHT LINE WINDS.

EF2 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1 MILE SOUTH OF BETSEY. THE TORNADO HAD A PATH LENGTH OF 1/4 MILE AND A WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED IS 110 TO 120 MPH. NUMEROUS TREES WERE DOWNED AND A BRICK HOME HAD PART OF ITS ROOF BLOWN OFF AND THE CORNER OF AN OUTSIDE WALL COLLAPSED.
HEAVY RAIN WASHED OUT A CULVERT ON ROUTE 1620.
MOUNTAIN PARKWAY STILL CLOSED IN BOTH DIRECTIONS.
KY 324...ONE LANE ON KY 174 AND KY 3303 ALL ARE CLOSED.
2 CALLS NEAR BLEVINS VALLEY REPORT MILL CREEK HAS PICKED UP SEVERAL VEHICLES. AND CARRIED THEM DOWNSTREAM. 1 WOMAN WAS RESCUED FROM HER CAR.
